rulings.Case Title: INTOUCH GROUP, INC., A CALIFORNIA COMPANY VS. ELIZABETH THIERIOT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Complainants' Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint To Allege Fraud With Specificity
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 30, 2010, line 13. Cross-Complainants' Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint To Allege Fraud With Specificity is granted on the condition that cross-complainant obtains a continuance of the trial date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: INTOUCH GROUP, INC., A CALIFORNIA COMPANY VS. ELIZABETH THIERIOT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 30, 2010, line 13. Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication. Summary judgment and summary adjudication denied. Triable issues of fact are raised in the 1st through 6th causes of action, burden of proof not shifted as to the 7th cause of action. Issue raised as to alter ego due to Kaplan's use of personal expenses on Intouch credit card (Ross deposition). Issue raised as to 1st and 2nd causes of action (fraud and violation of Corporations Code section 25401) where the email that accompanied the May P.P.M. stated it was the final and complete presentation to investors, but the September P.P.M. contained terms that were materially changed evidencing a possible misrepresentation. As to 3rd and 4th causes of action (conversion and money had and received) Thieriot testified she loaned money to Intouch wnd did not invest it. )Thieriot declaration paragraphs 15-17). As to the 5th cause of action (breach of contract) Thieriot testified that she performed under the agreement and was not paid. As to 6th cause of action (unlawful fraudulent transfer act) issue raised whether the $500,000 was a loan to "beef up" the balance sheet as Thieriot alleges, which would not entitle cross defendants to transfer this money to Kaplan. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MONICA DONG VS. DAMIEN QUINN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Void Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 30, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANT DAMIEN QUINN'S Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Void Default is granted. Defendant declares he was never properly served and the entry of default can be set aside at any time. C.C.P. section 473(d). No declaration presented from process server. A general policy exists that cases can be heard on the merits. The request for attorney fees is denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHASE BANK USA, N.A. VS. MICHELLE A RODRIGUEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 30, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT MICHELLE RODRIGUEZ'S Motion To Compel Arbitration is denied. Defendant has failed to plead a prior demand for arbitration under the agreement and a refusal to do so. C.C.P. section 1281.2. Further, it appears that neither of the forums in which the parties consented to arbitrate their disputes under the cardholder agreement are viable. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SQUARE MILE STRUCTURED DEBT (ONE) LLC et al VS. KENT M. SWIG
